$description: FIXED, non-themeable tokens for the WebAuthn authenticator and password manager UI. These values are baked into platform binaries at build time and signed. There is no runtime path to modify them — neither user-facing settings, nor the browser.theme API, nor sync. See components/authenticator/SECURITY_BOUNDARY.md for rationale.

brand.$description: A visually distinct palette the user cannot change. Chosen to be recognizable as 'the real authenticator' across OS vendors and to survive OS light/dark switches. Uses platform system colors where they provide anti-spoofing guarantees (system window chrome cannot be rendered by web content).

surface.$description: Uses native system window materials via platform-specific references. These resolve at build time to NSVisualEffectView (macOS), SystemBackdrop.Mica (Windows), Theme.Material3 (Android), system chrome (iOS), GTK theme-bg (Linux). The authenticator window is drawn by the browser process, not a renderer, and cannot be iframed or overlaid by web content.

identity.$description: Visual anti-spoof markers. Every authenticator window MUST display the webkitium lockmark (an SVG with this fill) and the bound relying-party origin in a fixed location. The lockmark asset ID is referenced by binary, not by path, so it cannot be replaced without re-signing.

lockmarkAsset.$description: Asset bundle index. Do not change.

type.$description: Uses platform system font at fixed HIG-standard sizes. Dynamic Type respected for accessibility but no user override of family.

rpOrigin.$description: Relying-party origin display size. Must be legible at minimum system font scale.

motion.$description: Fixed motion for the authenticator. No theme control — spoof attempts often use non-standard animation timing.
